burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; morbidity from hyperthyroidism and ophthalmopathy

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3; positive TRAb; thyroid uptake scan when needed

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with antithyroid therapy and follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Lab testing widely available; uptake scan in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antithyroid drugs (methimazole), beta-blockers, radioiodine or surgery when indicated; manage ophthalmopathy
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to specialized ophthalmology and radioiodine in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear diagnostic criteria and effective medical therapy
